text: The surgical team performs a robotic end-block radical nephrectomy and retrocaval lymphadenectomy on an elderly patient. The procedure begins with a transperitoneal approach, detaching the right colic to expose the surgical field. The inferior vena cava is dissected alongside the genital vein, providing access to the anastomosis of the contralateral left renal vein. The liver is mobilized, and hepatic veins are divided to enhance exposure of the operative area.

The surgeon progressively dissects the left renal vein, securing it with a loop. The renal artery is dissected and clamped via an interaortical caval approach on both sides of the left renal vein. The right renal vein is fully dissected and divided, exposing the major adenopathy. Retroperitoneal lymph nodes are detached step by step, starting from the anterior face, using a retro- and interaorticocaval axis.

The surgeon performs meticulous lymphostasis using hemostatic clips. The ureter is identified and divided. The kidney is detached at its inferior pole, along with perirenal fat and Gerota's fascia. An adrenalectomy is completed before detaching the kidney at its superior pole. A non-block intrafacial resection of the kidney and surrounding ganglion is executed.
